## FlowGuard Project - Comprehensive Analysis

I've completed a thorough exploration of the FlowGuard project. Here's the structured breakdown:

---

### 1. ROOT-LEVEL STRUCTURE

**Location:** `c:\Fullstack Assignment (FlowGuard)\FlowGuard`

**Top-level directories:**
- `client/` — React/Vite frontend
- `server/` — Node.js/Express backend
- `ai-service/` — Python FastAPI microservice (facial recognition & YOLO)
- `flowguard-ai/` — Documentation folders (team member outputs)
- `design/` — Architecture diagrams and design docs
- `docs/` — Implementation reports
- `.git/` — Repository metadata
- `.gitignore`, `README.md`, `deployment.md`

**Git context:**
- Current branch: `feature/incident-tracking` (at commit 9f2cb11)
- Main branch: at same commit (9f2cb11)
- Other feature branches diverge:
  - `origin/feature/object-detection-space` (badb731 - ahead)
  - `origin/feature/smart-logistics-whatsapp` (171517d - ahead)
  - `origin/feature/ai-helpdesk-support` (9f2cb11 - same)
  - `origin/feature/facial-recognition-access` (9f2cb11 - same)

---

### 2. SERVER/BACKEND (Node.js + Express)

**Location:** `c:\Fullstack Assignment (FlowGuard)\FlowGuard\server`

**Package.json dependencies:**
- Express 5.2.1, Cors 2.8.6, Dotenv 17.4.2
- Database: PostgreSQL (pg 8.20.0, Sequelize 6.37.8, pgvector 0.2.1, pg-hstore)
- Authentication: jsonwebtoken 9.0.3, bcrypt 6.0.0
- File uploads: multer 2.1.1, form-data 4.0.5
- HTTP: axios 1.16.0
- Validation: yup 1.7.1
- Test: jest 30.4.2, supertest 7.2.2

**Models (`server/models/`):**
- `User.js` — Roles: FM, Tenant, Staff; includes faceVector (ARRAY FLOAT), companyCode (Hybrid registration), managerId (staff-to-tenant link)
- `Attendance.js` — IN/OUT timestamps, linked to User
- `IncidentLog.js` — camera_location, status, person_name, confidence_score (paranoid soft-delete)
- `MonitoringZone.js` — zone_name, location, time_threshold
- `DetectionAlert.js` — zone/camera info, object_class, duration_seconds, person_name
- `SecurityLog.js` — Manual FM review workflow (reviewStatus, reviewNotes, reviewedBy, reviewedAt); severity levels
- `Booking.js` — booking_ref, transport_company, license_plate, driver_phone, loading_bay, status
- `Staff.js` — name, role, face_embedding (legacy?)
- `Invite.js` — One-time tenant registration codes, 48-hour expiry

**Routes (`server/routes/`):**

1. **`user.js`** — Authentication & user management
   - POST `/register` — Multi-level security: reCAPTCHA → role check → Invite validation (Tenant) or Hybrid code check (Staff)
   - POST `/login` — reCAPTCHA + JWT (1-hour expiry)
   - POST `/invite-tenant` — FM-only: generate 48h tenant invites
   - PUT `/generate-code` — Tenant refreshes staff registration code (resets usage to 0)
   - GET `/` — FM-only: all users with latest attendance status
   - GET `/:id` — Staff logs (FM-only, RBAC enforced)
   - PUT `/suspend/:id` — Toggle account active status
   - DELETE `/:id` — PDPA off-boarding (with self-deletion guard)
   - GET `/my-code` — Tenant's current registration code status
   - GET `/my-staff` — Tenant's registered staff members

2. **`incident.js`** — Incident/detection log CRUD
   - POST `/scan-frame` — AI integration: send CCTV frame → Python AI → save results (faces, unauthorized access alerts)
   - GET `/` — Search by name/location/status
   - GET `/:id`, DELETE `/:id`

3. **`zones.js`** — Monitoring zone CRUD
   - GET `/`, POST `/`, PUT `/:id`, DELETE `/:id`

4. **`detectionAlerts.js`** — Object detection alerts + 30-day purge
   - GET `/?status=Active`, POST `/`, PUT `/:id`
   - Auto-purge stale alerts (30+ days old) every 24h

5. **`security.js`** — FM security log review workflow (verifyToken required)
   - POST `/logs` — Auto-save from V-Patrol (severity: safe → auto-resolved; else → "Pending Review")
   - GET `/logs` — Dashboard queue with status filter
   - GET `/logs/personnel/:name`, `/logs/user/:id` — Personnel timeline
   - PATCH `/logs/:id/review` — FM manual triage (reviewStatus, reviewNotes)

6. **`booking.js`** — Logistics workflow
   - POST `/create` — Save booking + attempt WhatsApp (Meta template: `driver_booking_link`)
   - GET `/:ref`, `/all`

7. **`attendance.js`** — Clock-in/out RBAC
   - GET `/logs` — Role-based: FM sees all, Tenant sees own + their staff, Staff denied
   - POST `/scan` — Autonomous: name → User lookup → toggle IN/OUT for today

**Middlewares (`server/middlewares/`):**
- `auth.js` — `verifyToken` (JWT validation), `requireRole(...roles)` (RBAC)
- `errorHandlers.js` — Fallback 404 and 500 error handlers

**Config:**
- `.env.example` — Placeholder for DB, JWT_SECRET, reCAPTCHA, WhatsApp tokens, AI URLs
- `seed.js` — Bootstrap FM admin (admin@harrison.com / Admin123!)

---

### 3. CLIENT/FRONTEND (React + Vite)

**Location:** `c:\Fullstack Assignment (FlowGuard)\FlowGuard\client`

**Package.json dependencies:**
- React 19.2.5, React Router 7.14.2, Vite 8.0.10
- UI: MUI 5.18.0, Emotion (emotion/react, emotion/styled)
- Forms: Formik 2.4.9, Yup 1.7.1
- Auth: react-google-recaptcha-v3 1.11.0
- Other: axios 1.15.2, dayjs 1.11.20, react-qr-code 2.0.21
- Test: vitest 4.1.9, jest 30.4.2, jsdom 29.1.1

**Pages (`src/pages/`):**

| Page | Purpose | Auth | Role |
|------|---------|------|------|
| `Home.jsx` | Landing/marketing page | No | Public |
| `Login.jsx` | Email + password + reCAPTCHA | No | Public |
| `Register.jsx` | Multi-role registration (Tenant invite, Staff hybrid code, FM blocked) | No | Public |
| `ForgotKey.jsx` | Password reset flow | No | Public |
| `Dashboard.jsx` | Role-adaptive overview (FM sees global, Tenant sees unit) | Yes | Any |
| `FaceEnrollment.jsx` | Biometric 3-angle capture (camera or upload) → Python `/api/encode-faces` | Yes | Any |
| `VPatrol.jsx` | V-Patrol live facial recognition with liveness (head-turn validation), incident log timeline | Yes | FM + Staff |
| `GateScanner.jsx` | Attendance auto clock-in/out at gate (face recognition + `/api/attendance/scan`) | Yes | FM + Staff |
| `Cameras.jsx` | CCTV network overview (6 demo feeds) | Yes | FM + Staff |
| `ObjectDetection.jsx` | Zone creation, YOLO alerts, people counting, frame analysis | Yes | FM + Staff |
| `Attendance.jsx` | Workforce daily attendance metrics (RBAC: FM global, Tenant sees own staff) | Yes | FM + Tenant |
| `TenantLogistics.jsx` | Booking form + live driver portal invites (WhatsApp integration) | Yes | FM + Tenant |
| `DriverPortal.jsx` | Time-slot selection for delivery arrival | No | Public |
| `DriverPass.jsx` | QR code pass generation for driver gate access | No | Public |
| `StaffManagement.jsx` | Tenant's staff roster, company code (48h expiry), usage capacity (0–10) | Yes | Tenant only |
| `Users.jsx` | FM user directory with suspend/delete/re-enrollment actions | Yes | FM only |
| `SecurityReview.jsx` | FM manual log triage (Pending Review → False Positive/Escalated/Resolved) | Yes | FM only |
| `UserLogs.jsx` | Personnel activity timeline by user ID | Yes | FM only |
| `TenantManagement.jsx` | FM invite generation for new tenants | Yes | FM only |
| `Attendance.jsx` — alternate view | Same as above but role-specific | Yes | FM + Tenant |
| `Settings.jsx` | FM system configuration | Yes | FM only |
| `SystemHealth.jsx` | Uptime/AI service status | No | Public |
| `AIInnovation.jsx` | Feature showcase page | No | Public |
| `Contact.jsx` | Support contact form | No | Public |
| `NotFound.jsx`, `SystemError.jsx` | Error pages (404, 400, 401, 403, 500) | — | Public |

**Components (`src/components/`):**
- `Sidebar.jsx` — Role-aware navigation (FM + Staff see ops, Tenant sees staff+logistics, all see dashboard)
- `ProtectedRoute.jsx` — Auth guard + RBAC redirect (no token → 401, wrong role → 403)
- `NavBar.jsx` — Top navigation bar
- `ErrorBoundary.jsx` — React error fallback
- `AIChatPopup.jsx` — AI helpdesk chatbot (Lucas's module)
- `AIChatPopup.css` — Styling for chatbot
- `FeatureCards.jsx`, `Hero.jsx`, `ImpactStats.jsx`, `Roadmap.jsx`, `TechStack.jsx`, `Footer.jsx`, `LogoIcon.jsx`, `NodeCard.jsx`, `ContactForm.jsx`, `LiveStatus.jsx` — Landing page components

**Constants (`src/constants/`):**
- `roles.js` — Canonical role enums (FM, Staff, Tenant) and access lists (FM_ONLY, FM_STAFF, FM_TENANT, ANY)

**Styling:**
- `src/css/` — ~20 stylesheets (dark-mode enterprise design)
- Tailwind-like utility classes (e.g., `bg-[#0f172a]`, `min-h-screen`)

**Config:**
- `.env.example` — VITE_RECAPTCHA_SITE_KEY, VITE_API_URL
- `vite.config.js` — React + SWC
- `vitest.config.js` — Test runner config

---

### 4. DATABASE SETUP

**Technology:** PostgreSQL with Sequelize ORM

**Schema files:** None explicit; Sequelize auto-generates tables via `model.sync({ alter: true })`

**Tables:**
1. `users` — User accounts with faceVector (ARRAY FLOAT), RBAC roles
2. `attendance` — Clock-in/out logs with timestamps
3. `incident_logs` — Facial recognition detections (soft-delete with paranoid: true)
4. `monitoring_zones` — AI monitoring zones
5. `detection_alerts` — Object/person detection with auto-purge (30+ days)
6. `security_logs` — Manual FM review workflow (UUID id, reviewStatus states)
7. `bookings` — Logistics delivery scheduling
8. `staff_members` — Legacy staff embeddings
9. `invites` — One-time tenant registration codes

**Indexes/Features:**
- faceVector stored as FLOAT[] (native PostgreSQL array, no pgvector extension required)
- Soft-delete (paranoid: true) on logs/zones/alerts
- Hard-delete on User (paranoid: false)
- Composite unique constraints (email, booking_ref, invite code)
- Foreign keys with cascading deletes where appropriate

---

### 5. ENVIRONMENT/CONFIG

**Server `.env` variables:**
```
APP_PORT=3000 (or 5000)
APP_SECRET=jwt-signing-secret
CLIENT_URL=http://localhost:5173
DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_NAME, DB_USER, DB_PWD
FACE_AI_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8501 (InsightFace base URL)
PYTHON_AI_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8000/recognize (Incident scan endpoint)
RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY=google-secret-key
WHATSAPP_ACCESS_TOKEN, WHATSAPP_PHONE_NUMBER_ID (Meta API)
```

**Client `.env` variables:**
```
VITE_RECAPTCHA_SITE_KEY=google-site-key
VITE_API_URL=https://backend-url (prod); dev uses Vite proxy
```

**AI Service `.env` variables:**
```
DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_NAME, DB_USER, DB_PWD
FACE_MODEL_NAME=buffalo_l (default InsightFace model)
FACE_DET_SIZE=320 (detection resolution)
FACE_CTX_ID=-1 (CPU mode)
```

**Docker:** No Dockerfile present; deployment uses Vercel (frontend), Render (backend + AI)

---

### 6. SPECIAL INTEGRATIONS

**Facial Recognition (InsightFace + Python):**
- AI Service loads enrolled embeddings from PostgreSQL on startup
- POST `/api/encode-faces` — Captures 3 angles, averages embeddings, normalizes
- POST `/user/recognize` — Live CCTV → liveness check (head-turn ratio) → cosine similarity match
- V-Patrol (VPatrol.jsx) displays real-time security scan with liveness validation

**Object Detection (YOLO):**
- Ultralytics YOLOv8n model for people counting and object detection
- Endpoints: `/ai/api/yolo/people-count`, `/ai/api/yolo/analyze-frame`
- ObjectDetection.jsx manages zones and alerts from frame analysis

**Google reCAPTCHA v3:**
- Integrated on login and register forms
- Server-side verification with score threshold (0.5)

**WhatsApp Logistics Notifications:**
- Booking creation triggers Meta Graph API v19.0 template message
- Template name: `driver_booking_link` with custom parameter (booking URL)
- Fallback: logs error if WhatsApp unavailable

**AI Helpdesk (Lucas's module):**
- AIChatPopup component embedded in dashboard
- Separate AI service endpoint (not detailed in current files)

---

### 7. GIT BRANCH CONTEXT

**Current branch:** `feature/incident-tracking`

**All branches sync status:**
- `main` & `feature/incident-tracking` both at 9f2cb11 (Felicia's facial recognition PR)
- **Divergent branches** (different commits):
  - `origin/feature/object-detection-space` (badb731) — Charlisa's YOLO integration; latest commit "updated readme"
  - `origin/feature/smart-logistics-whatsapp` (171517d) — WhatsApp + RBAC; includes driver portal, logistics, role-based attendance
  - `origin/feature/ai-helpdesk-support` (9f2cb11) — Same as main (Lucas's AI chatbot)

**Team allocation:**
- **Gladwin** — Incident Tracking (`feature/incident-tracking` branch) — IncidentLog model, incident.js routes, post-frame scan
- **Felicia** — Facial Recognition (`feature/facial-recognition-access`) — User.isEnrolled, faceVector, FaceEnrollment, VPatrol, GateScanner, SecurityLogs/ReviewSecurity
- **Charlisa** — Object Detection (`feature/object-detection-space`) — MonitoringZone, DetectionAlert, YOLO integration, ObjectDetection.jsx
- **Lucas** — AI Helpdesk (`feature/ai-helpdesk-support`) — Chat service, knowledge base, FM ticketing
- **Smart Logistics** — WhatsApp notifications, DriverPortal, Booking, TenantLogistics (merged into main)

---

### KEY TECHNICAL HIGHLIGHTS

1. **RBAC Security:**
   - Canonical role enum in `server/middlewares/auth.js` and `client/src/constants/roles.js`
   - JWT token includes user ID, email, and role
   - Protected routes validate token + role; fallback 401/403 pages
   - Attendance and Security Log queries respect role compartmentalization (Tenant PDPA compliance)

2. **Hybrid Staff Registration:**
   - Tenants generate time-limited (48h) company codes with capacity limits (0–10 staff)
   - Staff registration validates employer's active code before linking

3. **Biometric Enrollment Flow:**
   - Multi-step: 3-angle webcam capture or file upload → Base64 transmission → Python InsightFace encoding → face vector storage in User.faceVector
   - Re-enrollment supported (SearchParams with userId + returnTo for post-enrollment redirect)

4. **AI Integration Patterns:**
   - Incident scan: CCTV frame → Python AI → save to IncidentLog
   - V-Patrol: Live webcam → InsightFace recognition + liveness check → SecurityLog (auto-resolved if safe, else pending FM review)
   - Gate Scanner: Fast clock-in/out using face recognition

5. **Manual FM Workflow:**
   - SecurityLog.reviewStatus states: Pending Review, False Positive, Escalated, Resolved
   - FM triages suspicious events in SecurityReview.jsx via PATCH `/api/security/logs/:id/review`

6. **Data Retention & Privacy:**
   - Paranoid soft-delete for logs/zones/alerts (can be restored)
   - Hard-delete for User (PDPA off-boarding)
   - 30-day auto-purge of stale detection alerts
   - FLOAT[] array for face vectors (no pgvector extension required)

---

**All file paths are absolute Windows paths under `c:\Fullstack Assignment (FlowGuard)\FlowGuard\`**

Module 4: Incident Tracking & Resolution
Focus: Centralized management and resolution tracking for security breaches (FR) and spatial anomalies (OD) detected by the AI.

Read (R): FM staff utilizes a centralized "Security Command Center" dashboard to fetch and read a sortable, filterable table of all active incidents. The dashboard categorizes incidents by severity, location, and the originating AI source (Facial Recognition vs. Object Detection).

Update (U): FM staff can update the status of an incident as it moves through the resolution pipeline (e.g., changing status from "Investigating" to "Escalated to Security" to "Cleared"). They can also append manual resolution notes to the AI's automated log (e.g., "Pallet was successfully relocated to Bay 2 by staff").

Delete (D): FM staff can permanently delete false-positive incidents generated by the AI to keep the analytics accurate. The system also includes a function to archive and remove resolved incidents from the active dashboard at the end of each month for compliance reporting.

Perfect! Now I have all the information you need. Let me compile a comprehensive summary of the FlowGuard routing and navigation structure:

---

## FlowGuard Routing & Navigation Structure

### **Route Definitions (App.jsx)**

**Key Route Paths & Access Control:**

| Path | Component | Allowed Roles | Purpose |
|------|-----------|---------------|---------|
| `/` | Home | PUBLIC | Landing page |
| `/login` | Login | PUBLIC | Authentication |
| `/register` | Register | PUBLIC | New user signup |
| `/dashboard` | Dashboard | ANY (`FM`, `Staff`, `Tenant`) | Role-adaptive command center |
| `/enrollment` | FaceEnrollment | ANY | Face registration (all users) |
| **Live Monitoring (FM + Security Staff)** | | | |
| `/cameras` | Cameras | `FM_STAFF` | Live camera feeds |
| `/object-detection` | ObjectDetection | `FM_STAFF` | AI object/threat detection |
| `/vpatrol` | VPatrol | `FM_STAFF` | Virtual patrol facial-recognition engine |
| `/gate-scanner` | GateScanner | `FM_STAFF` | Gate access scanning |
| **Attendance & Logistics (FM + Tenant)** | | | |
| `/attendance` | Attendance | `FM_TENANT` | Daily workforce attendance |
| `/logistics` | TenantLogistics | `FM_TENANT` | Logistics & bay management |
| `/staff` | StaffManagement | `FM_TENANT` | Tenant's own staff management |
| **FM-Only Administration** | | | |
| `/users` | Users | `FM_ONLY` | User & staff administration |
| `/security-review` | SecurityReview | `FM_ONLY` | Manual triage of suspicious access events |
| `/tenant-management` | TenantManagement | `FM_ONLY` | Tenant onboarding |
| `/user-logs/:id` | UserLogs | `FM_ONLY` | Individual user activity audit trail |
| `/settings` | Settings | `FM_ONLY` | System configuration |

**Access Control Enums:**
```javascript
FM_ONLY: ['FM']
FM_STAFF: ['FM', 'Staff']  // live monitoring / operations
FM_TENANT: ['FM', 'Tenant'] // attendance, own-unit logistics
```

---

### **Sidebar Navigation Structure (Sidebar.jsx)**

The sidebar dynamically renders nav items based on user role stored in `localStorage`:

```javascript
// Role checks:
const isFM = user.role === 'FM';
const isStaff = user.role === 'Staff';
const isTenant = user.role === 'Tenant';
```

**Sidebar Nav Links:**

1. **Everyone (authenticated):**
   - `NavLink to="/dashboard"` → Dashboard

2. **FM + Security Staff** (isFM || isStaff):
   - `NavLink to="/cameras"` → Cameras
   - `NavLink to="/vpatrol"` → V-Patrol
   - `NavLink to="/object-detection"` → Object Detection
   - `NavLink to="/gate-scanner"` → Gate Scanner

3. **FM + Tenant** (isFM || isTenant):
   - `NavLink to="/attendance"` → Daily Attendance
   - `NavLink to="/logistics"` → Logistics & Bays

4. **Tenant Only** (isTenant):
   - `NavLink to="/staff"` → My Staff

5. **FM Only** (isFM):
   - `NavLink to="/users"` → User Management
   - `NavLink to="/security-review"` → **Security Review**
   - `NavLink to="/tenant-management"` → Tenant Onboarding
   - `NavLink to="/settings"` → Settings

---

### **SecurityReview.jsx - Full Structure Reference**

**File Path:** `c:\Fullstack Assignment (FlowGuard)\FlowGuard\client\src\pages\SecurityReview.jsx`

**State Management:**
```javascript
const [logs, setLogs] = useState([]); // Array of security log objects
const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
const [filter, setFilter] = useState('Pending Review'); // Current filter status
const [drafts, setDrafts] = useState({}); // { [logId]: { reviewStatus, reviewNotes } }
const [savingId, setSavingId] = useState(null); // ID being saved (for loading state)
const [notification, setNotification] = useState(''); // Toast message
```

**Filter Options:**
```javascript
const REVIEW_STATUSES = ['Pending Review', 'False Positive', 'Escalated', 'Resolved'];
```

**API Endpoints Used:**
- **GET** `/api/security/logs?status={status}&limit=100` — Fetch logs by status
- **PATCH** `/api/security/logs/{id}/review` — Update review status & notes
  - Payload: `{ reviewStatus, reviewNotes }`

**Table Columns:**
1. `TIMESTAMP` — `log.time` or `log.createdAt` (formatted to Singapore locale)
2. `EVENT` — `log.icon + log.type`, description in `log.desc`
3. `PERSONNEL` — `log.personnelName` (shows "Unknown" if null)
4. `SEVERITY` — `log.severity` (badge: "safe" → green, others → red)
5. `REVIEW STATUS` — Dropdown select with `REVIEW_STATUSES` options; displays current badge
6. `RESOLUTION` — Textarea for `reviewNotes`; **Save Review** button to persist; shows `reviewedBy` if present

**Key JSX Features:**
- **Sidebar integration:** Layout uses `<Sidebar />` + `<main className="dashboard-main">`
- **Conditional rendering:** Loading spinner, "No logs match" empty state
- **Draft system:** Changes don't auto-save; user explicitly clicks "Save Review"
- **Toast notification:** Success/error messages appear for 4 seconds
- **Button states:** Disabled during save (`savingId === log.id`)

**Log Object Fields Expected:**
- `id` — Unique identifier
- `time` or `createdAt` — Timestamp
- `icon`, `type`, `desc` — Event metadata
- `personnelName` — Name of person involved
- `severity` — Alert level
- `reviewStatus` — Current review state
- `reviewNotes` — FM notes
- `reviewedBy` — FM who reviewed it

---

### **IncidentLog Model (server/models/IncidentLog.js)**

**Database Table:** `incident_logs`

**Fields:**
```javascript
camera_location: STRING(255)    // Required; e.g., "Sector A", "Gate 1"
status: STRING(50)              // Required; enum values: "AUTHORIZED_ACCESS", "UNAUTHORIZED_ACCESS", etc.
person_name: STRING(255)        // Optional; detected person's name from AI
confidence_score: DECIMAL(5,4)  // Optional; AI confidence (0.0000 to 1.0000)
id, createdAt, updatedAt, deletedAt // Sequelize defaults (paranoid soft-delete enabled)
```

**Status Enum Values (from incident.js):**
- `UNAUTHORIZED_ACCESS` — Triggers alert
- Other statuses implied by context (e.g., `AUTHORIZED_ACCESS`)

---

### **Incident API Endpoints (server/routes/incident.js)**

**GET /api/incident**
- Query params: `?search={text}` (searches person_name, camera_location, status)
- Returns: Array of `IncidentLog` records ordered by `createdAt DESC`

**GET /api/incident/:id**
- Returns: Single `IncidentLog` or 404

**POST /api/incident/scan-frame**
- Multipart form data: `file` (CCTV frame), `camera_location` (string)
- Calls Python AI service; saves detected faces to DB
- Returns: `{ success: true, ai_detections: [...], db_logs: [...] }`

**DELETE /api/incident/:id**
- Soft-deletes the log (paranoid mode)
- Returns: 200 OK

---

### **Key Implementation Notes**

1. **Sidebar icon/label structure:** Sidebar.jsx uses simple `<NavLink>` with text labels (no separate icon field). To add new items for FM, add to the `{isFM && {...}}` section with `<NavLink to="/path">Label</NavLink>`.

2. **Role-based routing:** `ProtectedRoute` wrapper enforces `allowedRoles` from `ACCESS` enum. If user tries to access a path outside their role, they're redirected to `/error/403`.

3. **SecurityReview vs. IncidentDashboard:** SecurityReview is for **manual review of flagged logs** (with reviewer notes). An IncidentDashboard would display **all incidents in real-time** with filtering/search. The structure is similar but purpose differs.

4. **Data flow for new incident page:**
   - Backend: Route GET `/api/incident?search=...` already exists
   - Sidebar: Add `<NavLink to="/incidents">Incident Dashboard</NavLink>` under `{isFM && {...}}`
   - Routes: Add `<Route path="/incidents" element={<ProtectedRoute allowedRoles={ACCESS.FM_ONLY}><IncidentDashboard /></ProtectedRoute>} />`
   - Component: Model SecurityReview's table/filter pattern but fetch from `/api/incident` and display real-time without review fields.

```javascript
FM_ONLY: ['FM']
FM_STAFF: ['FM', 'Staff']  // live monitoring / operations
FM_TENANT: ['FM', 'Tenant'] // attendance, own-unit logistics
```

---

### **Sidebar Navigation Structure (Sidebar.jsx)**

The sidebar dynamically renders nav items based on user role stored in `localStorage`:

```javascript
// Role checks:
const isFM = user.role === 'FM';
const isStaff = user.role === 'Staff';
const isTenant = user.role === 'Tenant';
```

1. **Everyone (authenticated):**
   - `NavLink to="/dashboard"` → Dashboard

2. **FM + Security Staff** (isFM || isStaff):
   - `NavLink to="/cameras"` → Cameras
   - `NavLink to="/vpatrol"` → V-Patrol
   - `NavLink to="/object-detection"` → Object Detection
   - `NavLink to="/gate-scanner"` → Gate Scanner

3. **FM + Tenant** (isFM || isTenant):
   - `NavLink to="/attendance"` → Daily Attendance
   - `NavLink to="/logistics"` → Logistics & Bays

4. **Tenant Only** (isTenant):
   - `NavLink to="/staff"` → My Staff

5. **FM Only** (isFM):
   - `NavLink to="/users"` → User Management
   - `NavLink to="/security-review"` → **Security Review**
   - `NavLink to="/tenant-management"` → Tenant Onboarding
   - `NavLink to="/settings"` → Settings

**File Path:** `c:\Fullstack Assignment (FlowGuard)\FlowGuard\client\src\pages\SecurityReview.jsx`

```javascript
const [logs, setLogs] = useState([]); // Array of security log objects
const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
const [filter, setFilter] = useState('Pending Review'); // Current filter status
const [drafts, setDrafts] = useState({}); // { [logId]: { reviewStatus, reviewNotes } }
const [savingId, setSavingId] = useState(null); // ID being saved (for loading state)
const [notification, setNotification] = useState(''); // Toast message
```

**Filter Options:**
```javascript
const REVIEW_STATUSES = ['Pending Review', 'False Positive', 'Escalated', 'Resolved'];
```

**API Endpoints Used:**
- **GET** `/api/security/logs?status={status}&limit=100` — Fetch logs by status
- **PATCH** `/api/security/logs/{id}/review` — Update review status & notes
  - Payload: `{ reviewStatus, reviewNotes }`

**Table Columns:**
1. `TIMESTAMP` — `log.time` or `log.createdAt` (formatted to Singapore locale)
2. `EVENT` — `log.icon + log.type`, description in `log.desc`
3. `PERSONNEL` — `log.personnelName` (shows "Unknown" if null)
4. `SEVERITY` — `log.severity` (badge: "safe" → green, others → red)

- **Conditional rendering:** Loading spinner, "No logs match" empty state

- **Toast notification:** Success/error messages appear for 4 seconds
- **Button states:** Disabled during save (`savingId === log.id`)

- `id` — Unique identifier
- `time` or `createdAt` — Timestamp
- `icon`, `type`, `desc` — Event metadata
- `personnelName` — Name of person involved
- `severity` — Alert level
- `reviewStatus` — Current review state
- `reviewNotes` — FM notes
- `reviewedBy` — FM who reviewed it

```javascript

status: STRING(50)              // Required; enum values: "AUTHORIZED_ACCESS", "UNAUTHORIZED_ACCESS", etc.
person_name: STRING(255)        // Optional; detected person's name from AI
confidence_score: DECIMAL(5,4)  // Optional; AI confidence (0.0000 to 1.0000)
id, createdAt, updatedAt, deletedAt // Sequelize defaults (paranoid soft-delete enabled)
```

- Query params: `?search={text}` (searches person_name, camera_location, status)

- Returns: 200 OK

```jsx
<Route

    </ProtectedRoute>
  }
```

```jsx
<NavLink to="/incidents">Incident Dashboard</NavLink>
```

### Header
- Title: **"Incident Command Center"**  
- Subtitle: `"AI-generated and manually logged security incidents"`  
- Styled like the header in `SecurityReview.jsx` and `Users.jsx` — flex row, space-between, margin-bottom 30px.

- **Severity dropdown** — All / Critical / High / Medium / Low

- `background: #0f172a`, `border-radius: 16px`, `border: 1px solid #1e293b`

- `tr:hover`: `background: rgba(59, 130, 246, 0.03)`

| Column | Source Field | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| TIMESTAMP | `createdAt` | Formatted: `toLocaleString('en-SG')` |
| LOCATION | `camera_location` | Plain text |
| PERSON | `person_name` | "Unknown" if null |
| AI SOURCE | `source` | Badge: blue for FR, purple for OD |

| SEVERITY | `severity` | Badge: Red=Critical, Orange=High, Yellow=Medium, Blue=Low |

Follow the `.status-badge` + `.role-*` pattern from `Users.css`:

| ProtectedRoute + ACCESS enum | `App.jsx`, `src/constants/roles.js` |

---

## Verification

1. Run `npm run dev` from `client/`
2. Log in as FM (admin@harrison.com / Admin123!)
3. Confirm "Incident Dashboard" link appears in the sidebar under the FM section

content.text:
**FlowGuard — Session Handoff Summary**
**Branch:** `feature/incident-tracking`
**Date:** 26 June 2026
**Developer:** Gladwin

---

**What was accomplished this session**

We built the Incident Tracking Dashboard page (the "Security Command Center") for Gladwin's module. This was purely a frontend, static-data session — no backend integration was implemented. The goal was to establish the full visual shell of the dashboard so real data wiring can be done in a future session.

---

**Files created**

`client/src/pages/IncidentDashboard.jsx`
The main page component. Uses local React state only — no API calls, no useEffect fetches. Contains 12 hardcoded static incident records with realistic field values (camera locations, person names, confidence scores, timestamps spread across 48 hours). Features: 4 summary stat cards (Total / Critical Active / Under Investigation / Cleared) whose counts react to the active filters; a filter bar with a text search (person name or location), severity dropdown, AI source dropdown, and resolution status dropdown — all filtered client-side via useMemo; a full incident table with 8 columns (Timestamp, Location, Person, AI Source, Confidence, Severity, Status, Actions); a read-only detail modal triggered by the View button; and a Delete button that removes the row from local state only (toast confirms, page refresh restores everything). Zero backend side effects.

`client/src/css/IncidentDashboard.css`
New stylesheet that extends Management.css and Users.css. Defines the 4-column stat card grid with coloured left-border accents, the filter bar inputs/selects, custom badge classes for severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low), AI source (Face ID / Object Det.), and resolution status (Active/Investigating/Escalated/Cleared), the wider detail modal layout, a two-column detail grid, the notes box, and the person avatar cell. Responsive breakpoints for 1024px, 768px, and 480px.

---

**Files modified**

`client/src/App.jsx`
Added import for IncidentDashboard and a new protected route at `/incidents` wrapped with `ACCESS.FM_ONLY`.

`client/src/components/Sidebar.jsx`
Added `<NavLink to="/incidents">Incident Dashboard</NavLink>` inside the `{isFM && ...}` block, positioned between Security Review and Tenant Onboarding.

---

**Design decisions made**

The page reuses the existing class names from Management.css (`table-container`, `management-table`, `edit-btn`, `modal-overlay`, `toast-notification`) and Users.css (`action-btn`, `action-neutral`, `action-danger`, `action-button-group`, `users-toast`) so it looks visually identical to SecurityReview and Users pages without duplicating styles. All new classes are namespaced with the `inc-` prefix to avoid collisions. The dashboard layout wrapper follows the same `dashboard-layout` + `dashboard-main` pattern used by every other authenticated page.

---

**What is NOT done yet (for the next session)**

The CRUD plan describes four capabilities that were intentionally left out:
1. **Create** — FM manual incident creation form (modal or separate page). The backend route `POST /api/incident/scan-frame` already exists for AI-triggered creation; manual FM creation via a form is not yet wired.
2. **Read (real data)** — Replace the static array with a real `useEffect` fetch to `GET /api/incident?search=...`. The route already exists and returns `incident_logs` ordered by `createdAt DESC` with optional search. The filter bar is already structured to pass params.
3. **Update** — Status pipeline progression (Active → Investigating → Escalated → Cleared) and appending resolution notes. The current modal is read-only; it needs an edit mode or inline controls similar to SecurityReview's draft/save pattern.
4. **Delete** — Wire the Delete button to call `DELETE /api/incident/:id` (soft-delete via paranoid mode) instead of just filtering local state. A confirmation modal before the API call would be good practice.

The static data fields (`id`, `camera_location`, `person_name`, `confidence_score`, `status`, `source`, `severity`, `resolutionStatus`, `notes`, `createdAt`) map directly to the real `IncidentLog` model columns, so swapping in live data should be straightforward — the `source` and `severity` fields will need to be either stored in the DB or derived on the backend when real creation is implemented.

---

**How to verify the current state**

Run `npm run dev` from `client/`, log in as the FM admin (`admin@harrison.com` / `Admin123!`), and "Incident Dashboard" will appear in the sidebar under Security Review. Navigate to `/incidents`. All 12 static rows load, filters and search work, the View modal opens with full detail, and Delete removes from view with a toast. Non-FM users hitting `/incidents` are redirected to `/error/403`.
